work_unit: AS-EC-017 fix: --bg mode process group leak
situation: audit-2026-07-02-002 finding AS-EC-017 (priority 17, pending): autonomous-loop.sh --bg mode uses nohup which does not create a new session/process group. The backgrounded process remains in the terminal's process group, so trap's `kill 0` on SIGINT/SIGTERM can leak signals to sibling processes in the same terminal process group.
action: Replaced `nohup` with `setsid` in the --bg branch (line 40). setsid creates a new session + new process group + detaches from controlling terminal. This ensures the daemonized loop runs in its own process group; trap's `kill 0` only affects its own children, never leaking to the parent terminal's process group. IO redirection already handles stdin/stdout/stderr (</dev/null >LOG 2>&1), so nohup's signal-ignore side effect is unnecessary.
outcome_evidence: bash -n syntax check passed; opt-worktree.sh committed to opt-engine-shift-1782938888 (direction-shift triggered by public-interfaces.txt match on scripts/autonomous-loop.sh); pushed to origin/auto/opt-engine-shift-1782938888
next_suggestion: 派生 AS-EC-018 fix (opt-worktree.sh realpath -m symlink path rejection)，最后一个 pending finding；完成后 audit-cycle-state.status → cycle-complete，下轮触发新全量审计
